content:
{
  "title": "Cross-Lingual Semantic Anchor Prompting for Low-Resource Language Tasks",
  "problem": "Large language models often underperform on multilingual tasks, especially for low-resource languages, due to limited training data and insufficient cross-lingual transfer. Current prompting techniques do not explicitly encourage semantic consistency across languages.",
  "motivation": "Improving LLM performance on low-resource languages is crucial for global inclusivity and access to language technology. Prompting approaches that effectively bridge high- and low-resource languages could rapidly advance capabilities without additional model training, especially given recent LLM deployment in diverse linguistic contexts.",
  "method": "We propose Cross-Lingual Semantic Anchor Prompting (CLS-AP): for any low-resource input, we prepend semantically aligned anchor phrases in a high-resource language (e.g., English) alongside a translation of the same anchor in the target language. For example, for a sentiment analysis task, prompts would include 'This text expresses a positive feeling. / Este texto expresa un sentimiento positivo.' before the actual low-resource text. This method aims to reinforce the model's semantic mapping between languages and provide context for disambiguation. Anchors are selected from parallel corpora or generated using automatic translation with human verification.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate CLS-AP on XNLI and FLORES-101 datasets using languages with varying resource levels. Baselines will include standard prompts and translation-based pipelines. Metrics will be accuracy for classification and BLEU for generation. Statistical significance will be assessed via bootstrap resampling."
}
